text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village nestled between rolling hills and tranquil streams lived a curious woodmouse named Mittens.

Every morning Mittens wouldn’t wake up without nibbling on a shiny yellow feather from her favorite branches. She’d flutter from branch to branch, spreading feathers like glitter across her tail. People smiled. Children giggled.

One day, while chirping at dawn’s own spotlight, Mittens accidentally fluttered too low onto the path outside Mr. Fluffums’.

Mr. Fluffums’, who loved kittens more than anything else, leaped forwards. “Oh dear!” he squeaked. “That was no mere feather! That’s my long-unused sock!”

Mittens flew away faster than an owl thief. That evening she tucked into some nice soft moches.

That night an old hawk swooped down. Mittens froze. The hawk saw feathers. Not just any feathers — hers!

“Aha!” the hawk croaked. “You’re mine!” Then, perhaps
